title: Question: Verilator best practice flow - Large designs bottom-up?
body:
---


Author Name: **Yariv Saliternik**
Original Redmine Message: 84 from https://www.veripool.org



---

Hi,

My design size is around 5M gate. 
I run the verilator on the top level module and I get an obj_dir of 278MB (where the top level CPP file is around 190MB and there are 36 files).
When I try to compile with GCC (using the make file the verilator generates) it takes several hours and at the end it fails.

I have added to the verilator run the --output-split 20000 option and recieved the same project spilited to 300 files with the largest file 35MB (I am currently trying to compile it).

What I would like to know is what is the better way to run verilator on a large design? Should I try to run bottom up or should I stay with a flat run from the top to the bottom?

Thanks you,
Yariv.
